input_data.code:
password = "secret123" query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}" os.system(f"rm {filename}") return f"

{message}

"

findings.title: Hardcoded Password
findings.description: Password is hardcoded in source code, making it visible to anyone with access to the code
findings.fix: Store password in environment variables or secure configuration files

findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.description: User input 'user_id' is directly interpolated into SQL query without sanitization, allowing SQL injection attacks
findings.fix: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of string formatting

findings.title: OS Command Injection
findings.description: User-controlled 'filename' variable is directly passed to os.system without sanitization, allowing command injection
findings.fix: Use subprocess with shell=False and validate/sanitize filename input, or use safer file operations

findings.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
findings.description: User input 'message' is directly embedded in HTML output without escaping, allowing XSS attacks
findings.fix: HTML-encode the message content before embedding in HTML or use templating engines with auto-escaping
